scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with default inode and dir-index features

reopen.expected_state: rename is visible before unlink cleanup; after cleanup both old and renamed file paths are absent

expected_outcome.detail: small synchronous writes survive readback and rename before unlink cleanup

scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with two writable subdirectories

reopen.expected_state: fresh open observes link, symlink, and setattr results after directory cleanup

expected_outcome.detail: parallel metadata churn preserves link targets and directory cleanup ordering

scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with user_xattr enabled

reopen.expected_state: image reopen preserves sparse size, final fsync data boundary, and user.ffs_matrix xattr

expected_outcome.detail: sparse preallocation, truncate growth, xattrs, and final fsync are visible after readback

scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with read-write mount

reopen.expected_state: image reopen confirms rejected mknod created no device node and preserved preexisting file bytes

expected_outcome.detail: unsupported device node creation rejects deterministically and leaves the preexisting file unchanged

scenarios.image_setup: fresh single-device btrfs image with default subvolume

reopen.expected_state: btrfs rename is visible before unlink cleanup; after cleanup old and renamed paths are absent

expected_outcome.detail: btrfs mounted write/readback and rename follow the same durability envelope as ext4

scenarios.image_setup: fresh single-device btrfs image with writable subvolume

reopen.expected_state: fresh btrfs open observes link, symlink, and setattr results after directory cleanup

expected_outcome.detail: parallel btrfs metadata churn preserves link and directory invariants

scenarios.image_setup: fresh single-device btrfs image with xattr support

reopen.expected_state: btrfs image reopen preserves sparse extent state, final fsync data boundary, and user.ffs_matrix xattr

expected_outcome.detail: btrfs sparse write path keeps extents and xattr visibility coherent

scenarios.image_setup: root-owned btrfs test directory mounted with default_permissions

reopen.expected_state: image reopen confirms denied root-owned write left no probe file and preserved existing data

expected_outcome.detail: root-owned DefaultPermissions write rejects with no partial mutation and records host-vs-product classification

scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with user_xattr enabled and empty user.ffs_matrix namespace

reopen.expected_state: fresh open observes user.ffs_matrix after create, replace, list, and get checks

expected_outcome.detail: xattr create, replace, list, and get semantics preserve the final user.ffs_matrix value

scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with user_xattr enabled and an overlong user xattr name probe

reopen.expected_state: image reopen confirms the overlong xattr name was rejected without creating an xattr value

expected_outcome.detail: overlong xattr names reject deterministically and leave no xattr mutation behind

scenarios.image_setup: fresh single-device btrfs image with writable regular file extent state

reopen.expected_state: image reopen preserves file identity after keep-size prealloc, zero-range, punch-hole, and fsync

expected_outcome.detail: supported fallocate modes mutate only the requested file ranges and remain visible after reopen

scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image mounted read-only with default_permissions

reopen.expected_state: image reopen confirms read-only write probe produced no file and preserved the fixture root

expected_outcome.detail: read-only mounted write is rejected with EROFS and creates no partial probe path

scenarios.image_setup: btrfs image with repair-write route disabled before serialization

reopen.expected_state: image reopen confirms repair-write refusal happened before serialization and preserved preexisting data

expected_outcome.detail: read-write repair serialization path refuses unsupported mutation before any repair writeback probe is created

scenarios.image_setup: host capability probe lane for FUSE mount prerequisites

reopen.expected_state: host capability skip records missing FUSE prerequisites without claiming product pass or failure

expected_outcome.detail: missing host mount capability is classified as HOST_CAPABILITY_SKIP with preserved diagnostics

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with /parent/src and /parent/dst regular files

reopen.expected_state: parent fsyncdir makes overwrite rename durable with only destination surviving

expected_outcome.detail: overwrite rename survives parent fsyncdir and reopen with no split-brain src/dst state

reopen.expected_state: data fsync plus parent fsyncdir preserves renamed file data at the new path

expected_outcome.detail: non-overwrite rename survives data fsync and parent fsyncdir at the renamed path

reopen.expected_state: clean unmount preserves cross-directory rename and parent directory states

expected_outcome.detail: cross-directory rename keeps exactly one visible child after clean unmount and reopen

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with /links/original and no alias

reopen.expected_state: fresh stat observes both hardlink names and shared link count two

expected_outcome.detail: hardlink lifecycle records shared link count and both path survivors after close/open

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: btrfs image with /target/data and new symlink /sym

reopen.expected_state: forced reopen preserves symlink entry and target path bytes

expected_outcome.detail: symlink persists through forced reopen with target unchanged

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with /open/live held open across unlink

reopen.expected_state: open handle remains readable until close, then reopened namespace keeps path absent

expected_outcome.detail: unlink while open records nlink zero and no path resurrection after close/open

reopen.expected_state: rmdir rejection preserves both parent directory and child after reopen

expected_outcome.detail: non-empty rmdir rejection preserves child namespace and link counts

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with user_xattr enabled on /meta/file

reopen.expected_state: xattr update and parent directory fsyncdir keep file path and user xattr visible

expected_outcome.detail: xattr namespace update preserves path, link count, and xattr evidence after reopen

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: btrfs image with /alloc/file and unsupported fallocate mode requested

reopen.expected_state: unsupported fallocate refusal leaves file size, link count, and directory entry unchanged

expected_outcome.detail: unsupported fallocate mode is a no-op refusal with unchanged namespace state

namespace_scenarios.image_setup: host capability probe lane for namespace durability mounted smoke

reopen.expected_state: host capability skip records missing FUSE prerequisites without product pass/fail

expected_outcome.detail: missing host mount capability emits structured namespace durability skip artifacts

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: fresh ext4 image with single regular file pre-populated to 64 KiB

reopen.expected_state: post_fsync writer bytes survive reopen

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: single-device btrfs subvolume with file pre-populated to 64 KiB

reopen.expected_state: btrfs reader observes csum-verified post-fsync content

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with file f1 pre-populated to 32 KiB

reopen.expected_state: after holder closes inode is reaped and path remains absent

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with /src pre-populated and /dst absent

reopen.expected_state: reopen of /dst observes writes performed via h_open_src

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: btrfs subvolume with file pre-populated to 1 MiB

reopen.expected_state: reopened size matches post-truncate length
fresh open observes mode 0600

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with user_xattr enabled and /xattrfile pre-populated

reopen.expected_state: fresh open finds no user.k1 xattr

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: btrfs subvolume with /dir containing a, b, c entries

reopen.expected_state: reopen of /dir lists a and c only
reopened symlink at new path resolves to original target

multi_handle_scenarios.image_setup: ext4 image with read-only inode immutable flag set on /immut

reopen.expected_state: image reopen confirms file bytes equal pre-attempt content
